title: Code Llama
description: A code-specialized open-weight Llama line that made infilling and code completion a first-class part of the family.
openingSummary: Code Llama matters because it gave the Llama ecosystem a model line aimed directly at programming work instead of leaving code assistance as a side effect of general language modeling. The important idea is that it keeps the familiar decoder-only recipe while shifting the page toward infilling, source-code context, and developer workflows.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Code Llama is Meta's code-focused branch of the Llama family. In this atlas it works as a code-model checkpoint page: a named line that still behaves like a language model, but is packaged and tuned for software tasks such as completion, editing, and code explanation.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: The inputs are tokenized prompts that can mix natural-language instructions with code context. The outputs are token-by-token continuations, which may be full code blocks, inserted spans, or explanatory text around a patch, depending on how the calling system frames the request.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: Code Llama remains easiest to understand through decoder-only models, autoregressive generation, and the broader Transformer family. What makes it code-oriented is not a wholly new architecture class, but that the Llama stack is adapted for source-code context and fill-in-the-middle style behavior that matters in editing workflows.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: Attention still carries the context, and grouped-query attention still matters because Code Llama inherits the broader Llama-era push toward efficient large-context serving. The more practical difference for readers is that these modules now support code completion and infilling workflows instead of only general prose generation.

training.title: Training
training.body: Public Code Llama material emphasizes continued specialization for programming tasks on top of the Llama line, with variants for general code, Python-heavy use, and instruction following. This atlas slice keeps the training shell visible even when structured dataset and training-regime records are not fully expanded yet.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: Code Llama is a good bridge page for readers who already know Llama as a general family and want to understand what changes when the same broad architecture is aimed at developers. It should point readers back to language-model basics and over to the code-repair-models category rather than pretending coding models need a separate theory of generation.
